chronology.event: Purchase and Sale Agreement effective; price $1,275,000, closing set for April 10, 2026.
Addendum No. 1 (ADD-4412-001) executed; confirms April 10, 2026 closing and adds $7,500 seller credit.
Title commitment GT-TC-58821-A effective; discloses $4,800 code-enforcement lien (Schedule B-II Exception 7).
Property inspection performed; active roof leak above Unit 2C documented (OCR-only).
Realtor Tessa Lindqvist emails buyer referencing an (incorrect) March 31 closing and seller 'cold feet'.
Lender issues written loan commitment TBFCU-LC-99214 ($956,250); email also asserts March 31, 2026 closing (contradicts PSA).
Contractual closing date under PSA §9.1 and Addendum No. 1.

expectedAuthorityNames: DK Arena, Inc. v. EB Acquisitions I, LLC, 112 So. 3d 85 (Fla. 2013)
Microdecisions, Inc. v. Skinner, 889 So. 2d 871 (Fla. 2d DCA 2004)
courtListener.jurisdiction: Florida (Supreme Court of Florida and District Courts of Appeal); 13th Judicial Circuit, Hillsborough County trial venue
courtListener.legalIssue: Whether a buyer may obtain specific performance of a definite, signed Florida real-estate purchase and sale agreement where financing and inspection contingencies were timely satisfied and the seller repudiated, and the related question of whether recorded contract/property records implicate public-records access rather than copyright restriction.

qa.question: What is the total purchase price for 4412 Bayshore Blvd?

qa.expectedAnswer: $63,750.00, held by Gulfstream Title & Escrow, LLC under escrow file GT-58821.
qa.question: How much earnest money was deposited and who holds it?
What is the financing/inspection contingency deadline?

qa.expectedAnswer: Yes. The executed PSA (§9.1) and Addendum No. 1 set the closing date as April 10, 2026, but the loan officer's financing email (and the realtor .msg) state the closing date as March 31, 2026. The PSA/Addendum is the controlling written agreement; the emails are inconsistent.
qa.locatorHint: Compare email subject/body (March 31, 2026) against PSA §9.1 and Addendum No. 1 (April 10, 2026)
qa.question: Is there a contradiction in the documents about the closing date, and what is it?

qa.expectedAnswer: Yes. Commitment TBFCU-LC-99214 for $956,250.00 was issued March 11, 2026, which is before the March 14, 2026 contingency deadline.
qa.locatorHint: Email body and attached loan-commitment-TBFCU-LC-99214.pdf, compared to PSA §7.2
qa.question: Was the buyer's written loan commitment issued before the contingency deadline?

qa.expectedAnswer: An active roof leak above second-floor commercial Unit 2C, with water staining and soft decking, estimated to cost $18,500 to repair.
qa.locatorHint: Section 2 (Roofing) — only recoverable via OCR of the scanned report
qa.question: What material roof defect did the inspection identify, and what is the estimated repair cost?

qa.expectedAnswer: Raymond Ostrander of Bayfront Property Inspections, Florida inspector license No. HI-7741.
qa.question: Who performed the inspection and under what license number?

qa.expectedAnswer: The closing statement shows a $4,800.00 'Undisclosed Lien Payoff — Sunstate Code Enforcement' debited to Seller. This matches Schedule B-II, Exception 7 of the title commitment: a City of Tampa / Sunstate Code Enforcement lien recorded at O.R. Book 28114, Page 0042, for $4,800.00.
qa.locatorHint: Settlement sheet 'Undisclosed Lien Payoff' line; cross-reference title-commitment Schedule B-II Exception 7
qa.question: What undisclosed lien appears on the closing statement, and does it match a title exception?

qa.locatorHint: Settlement sheet 'ESTIMATED CASH TO CLOSE FROM BUYER' line
qa.question: What is the estimated cash to close from the buyer?

qa.expectedAnswer: A $7,500.00 credit toward the buyer's closing costs (Addendum Control No. ADD-4412-001, dated March 4, 2026).
qa.question: What closing-cost credit did the seller agree to in Addendum No. 1?

qa.expectedAnswer: Lot 12, Block 4, Bayshore Gardens Unit Two, per Plat Book 41, Page 18, Public Records of Hillsborough County, Florida.
qa.question: What is the recorded legal description of the property?

qa.expectedAnswer: NOT SUPPORTED — the documents do not answer this.
qa.locatorHint: No HOA membership or fee is stated in any document; refusal expected.
qa.question: What is the buyer's homeowners' association membership or HOA monthly fee for the property?

chronology.expectedAnswer: 2026-02-27 PSA; 03-04 addendum; 03-05 title; 03-06 inspection; 03-11 loan commitment; 03-14 contingency deadline; 04-10 closing.
chronology.prompt: Order execution, addendum, title, inspection, loan commitment, contingency deadline, and closing.

comparisons.expectedAnswer: The PSA and Addendum set April 10, 2026; the lender email incorrectly says March 31, 2026.
comparisons.prompt: Compare the contractual closing date with the email's asserted date.

contradictions.expectedAnswer: Controlling contract documents state April 10, while the lender email states March 31.

lists.expectedAnswer: $7,500 seller closing-cost credit and $4,800 code-enforcement lien payoff.
lists.prompt: List the material closing adjustments disclosed in the corpus.

negatives.expectedAnswer: Not supported; the corpus gives no HOA membership or fee.
negatives.prompt: What HOA fee applies to the property?

structures.expectedAnswer: $262,750 in the ESTIMATED CASH TO CLOSE FROM BUYER row.
structures.prompt: Resolve the buyer's estimated cash to close using spreadsheet labels.

versions.expectedAnswer: The executed PSA as supplemented by Addendum No. 1 controls; the unsupported MSG and inconsistent lender email do not amend it.
versions.prompt: Which documents control the closing date and seller credit?
